free_text: Extension of the excitation functions of deuteron
induced reactions on natSn up to 50 MeV
Natural high purity metallic Sn foils
One stack
consisting of Al (50um), Sn (55.4 um) and Cd (15 um)
foils irradiated with 50MeV deuteron beam of 50 nA for
2400s. The beam intensity was adopted from the
monitor reactions.
The Al foils served for monitoring and as catcher
foils. Activity of the foils was measured several times
to follow the decay of the reaction products.
No chemical separation was applied.
Several series of spectra were recorded to
be able to follow the decay.
Decay data were taken from NUDAT2.6 online database.
Energy degradation was calculated by using the semi
empirical formula of Andersen and Ziegler.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of stopping power of
each foil due to thickness variation and  straggling
effects. Uncertainty of the primary incident beam was
+- 0.2MeV which increased up to +-1.5MeV in the last
target foil of the stack.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the  standard way as the square root
of the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters. Uncertainty of time parameters
is not included. Overall uncertainty were about 8-13%.
surface density of target nuclei(3%),
incident charged particle flux (5%),
detector efficiency (5%)
nuclear decay and branching data(1-5%)
statistical and peak fitting (1-5%)
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes TALYS.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
